hyperedges.label: PreToolUse hook + permission rule deny-first precedence

nodes.label: Hook lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Events fall into three cadences: once per session, once per turn, and on every tool call inside the agentic loop. Hooks give deterministic control over Claude Code so certain actions always happen rather than relying on the LLM to choose to run them.

nodes.label: PreToolUse hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires before a tool call executes and can block it. Fires before any permission-mode check, so a hook returning permissionDecision deny blocks the tool even in bypassPermissions mode — letting policy be enforced that users cannot bypass. Returning allow does not bypass deny rules; hooks can tighten restrictions but not loosen them.

nodes.label: PostToolUse hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires after a tool call succeeds and cannot block or undo actions since the tool already executed; exit 2 only shows stderr to Claude.

nodes.label: PermissionRequest hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a permission dialog appears; can auto-approve via hookSpecificOutput.decision.behavior allow. Does not fire in non-interactive (-p) mode; use PreToolUse there instead.

nodes.label: PermissionDenied hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a tool call is denied by the auto mode classifier; return retry:true to tell the model it may retry. Denial already occurred so exit code and stderr are ignored.

nodes.label: SessionStart hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a session begins or resumes; stdout is added to Claude's context. Only event that receives a model field.

nodes.label: UserPromptSubmit hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a prompt is submitted before Claude processes it; stdout adds context, use additionalContext to inject text. Lowers command/http/mcp_tool timeout default to 30s. No matcher support.

nodes.label: Stop hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires whenever Claude finishes responding, not only at task completion, and not on user interrupts. Exit 2 prevents Claude from stopping. Overridden after blocking 8 times in a row; parse stop_hook_active to avoid loops, raise cap with CLAUDE_CODE_STOP_HOOK_BLOCK_CAP.

nodes.label: SubagentStop hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a subagent finishes; for subagents, Stop hooks are automatically converted to SubagentStop.

nodes.label: ConfigChange hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a configuration file changes during a session; matcher filters by source (user_settings, project_settings, local_settings, policy_settings, skills). Blocks the change except policy_settings.

nodes.label: CwdChanged hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when the working directory changes; useful for reactive environment management with tools like direnv via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE. No matcher support.

nodes.label: FileChanged hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a watched file changes on disk; matcher value is split into literal filenames rather than evaluated as a regex, unlike other events.

nodes.label: WorktreeCreate hook event
nodes.rationale: Replaces the default git worktree logic entirely, enabling non-git VCS (SVN, Perforce, Mercurial) or custom worktree locations. Any non-zero exit code aborts worktree creation — the only event where exit 1 blocks.

nodes.label: WorktreeRemove hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when a worktree is removed at session exit or subagent finish; pair with WorktreeCreate for custom cleanup.

nodes.label: Elicitation hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when an MCP server requests user input during a tool call; matcher filters by MCP server name. Can auto-respond to elicitation requests without showing a dialog.

nodes.label: Notification hook event
nodes.rationale: Fires when Claude Code sends a notification; matcher filters by type (permission_prompt, idle_prompt, auth_success, elicitation_dialog, etc). Cannot block; exit 2 shows stderr to user only.

nodes.label: Matcher patterns
nodes.rationale: Filters when hooks fire at the group level. Letters/digits/underscore/pipe are exact strings; any other character makes it a JS regex. MCP tools need .* appended because a bare mcp__server is treated as an exact string and matches nothing. FileChanged does not follow these rules.

nodes.label: if field (hook argument filter)
nodes.rationale: Filters hooks by tool name and arguments together using permission rule syntax, so the hook process only spawns when the tool call matches — avoiding process-spawn overhead beyond what matcher (tool-name only) offers. Holds exactly one rule with no &&/|| combining. Only works on tool events; requires v2.1.85+.

nodes.label: Hook exit code output
nodes.rationale: Exit 0 = success (stdout parsed for JSON; added as context for UserPromptSubmit/UserPromptExpansion/SessionStart). Exit 2 = blocking error, stderr fed to Claude. Only exit 2 blocks for most events because Claude Code treats exit 1 as non-blocking even though 1 is the conventional Unix failure code. WorktreeCreate is the exception where any non-zero aborts.

nodes.label: Structured JSON output
nodes.rationale: Exit 0 and print JSON for finer-grained control than exit codes. Must not mix with exit 2 — Claude Code ignores JSON when you exit 2 — so you choose one signaling approach per hook. Output strings capped at 10,000 chars.

nodes.label: Decision control (permissionDecision)
nodes.rationale: PreToolUse values allow/deny/ask (plus defer in -p mode). allow skips the interactive prompt but does not override deny/ask rules including managed deny lists, preserving deny-first precedence. Different events use different decision patterns (PostToolUse/Stop top-level decision:block; PermissionRequest hookSpecificOutput.decision.behavior).

nodes.label: Command hook type
nodes.rationale: Runs a shell command; receives event JSON on stdin and communicates via exit codes/stdout/stderr. Cannot trigger / commands or tool calls.

nodes.label: HTTP hook type
nodes.rationale: POSTs event JSON to a URL; response body uses the same JSON output format. HTTP status codes alone cannot block — must return 2xx with decision fields. Header env interpolation only resolves vars listed in allowedEnvVars.

nodes.label: MCP tool hook type
nodes.rationale: Calls a tool on an already-connected MCP server; never triggers an OAuth or connection flow. SessionStart/Setup typically fire before servers connect, so expect not-connected error on first run.

nodes.label: Prompt-based hook type
nodes.rationale: Single-turn LLM evaluation for decisions needing judgment rather than deterministic rules. Model (Haiku by default) returns ok:true/false with reason. 30s timeout. Use when hook input data alone is enough to decide.

nodes.label: Agent-based hook type
nodes.rationale: Experimental. Spawns a subagent that can read files, search code, and use tools to verify conditions against actual codebase state before returning ok/reason. 60s default timeout, up to 50 tool-use turns. Prefer command hooks for production.

nodes.label: Exec form and shell form
nodes.rationale: args present = exec form (spawned directly, no shell, no tokenization, path placeholders pass verbatim — preferred for path placeholders to avoid quoting). args absent = shell form (sh -c / Git Bash / PowerShell, supports pipes and &&). Windows exec form needs a real .exe, not .cmd/.bat shims.

nodes.label: Path placeholders (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR / PLUGIN_ROOT / PLUGIN_DATA)
nodes.rationale: Reference scripts relative to project or plugin root regardless of cwd. Exported as env vars to spawned processes. CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT changes on each plugin update; CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA persists across updates.

nodes.label: CLAUDE_ENV_FILE
nodes.rationale: A file Claude Code runs as a script preamble before each Bash command; SessionStart + CwdChanged hooks write to it to reload direnv/devbox environment per directory, which the Bash tool otherwise does not pick up.

nodes.label: Common input fields
nodes.rationale: session_id, transcript_path, cwd, permission_mode, effort, hook_event_name shared across events; agent_id/agent_type added inside subagents to distinguish subagent hook calls from main-thread calls.

nodes.label: /hooks menu
nodes.rationale: Read-only browser for configured hooks grouped by event, showing type prefix and source (User/Project/Local/Plugin/Session/Built-in). To add/modify/remove, edit settings JSON directly.

nodes.label: disableAllHooks
nodes.rationale: Temporarily disables all hooks without removing them; respects managed settings hierarchy — only set at the managed level can disable managed hooks. There is no way to disable an individual hook.

nodes.label: Hooks in skills and agents (frontmatter)
nodes.rationale: Hooks defined in skill/subagent YAML frontmatter are scoped to the component lifecycle and cleaned up when it finishes. once:true is only honored in skill frontmatter.

nodes.label: Combine results from multiple hooks
nodes.rationale: All matching hooks run in parallel to completion before merging; identical commands deduplicated. One hook's deny does not stop siblings, so don't rely on it to suppress side effects. Most restrictive answer wins: deny > ask > allow. additionalContext kept from every hook.

nodes.label: Permission modes
nodes.rationale: default/acceptEdits/plan/auto/dontAsk/bypassPermissions trade convenience for oversight. In every mode except bypassPermissions, writes to protected paths are never auto-approved. Modes set the baseline; permission rules layer on top.

nodes.label: Auto mode classifier
nodes.rationale: A separate classifier model reviews actions in fixed decision order: allow/deny rules resolve first, then working-dir reads/edits auto-approve except protected paths, then everything else goes to the classifier. Tool results are stripped so hostile file content cannot manipulate it directly. PermissionDenied hook fires on classifier denials.

nodes.label: Protected paths
nodes.rationale: Writes to .git/.vscode/.idea/.husky/.cargo/.claude and shell/git config files are never auto-approved except in bypassPermissions, preventing accidental corruption of repository state and Claude's own configuration.

nodes.label: Permission rule syntax
nodes.rationale: Format Tool or Tool(specifier). Bash glob * spans multiple args; space before * enforces a word boundary. Reused by the hook if field. Compound commands evaluated per-subcommand because Claude Code is aware of shell operators.

nodes.label: Manage permissions (deny-first precedence)
nodes.rationale: Rules evaluated deny -> ask -> allow; first matching wins so deny always takes precedence. A bare tool name deny removes the tool from context entirely; a scoped deny leaves it available and blocks matching calls. Enforced by Claude Code, not the model.

nodes.label: Extend permissions with hooks
nodes.rationale: PreToolUse hooks run before the permission prompt and can deny/force-prompt/skip. Hook decisions do not bypass deny/ask rules, preserving deny-first precedence including managed deny lists. A blocking hook (exit 2) takes precedence over allow rules and runs before rules are evaluated.

nodes.label: Settings files and scopes
nodes.rationale: User/Project/Local/Managed scopes determine where hooks and config apply. Hook location determines scope and shareability. Managed settings cannot be overridden by user or project.

nodes.label: Settings precedence
nodes.rationale: Managed > CLI args > Local > Project > User. Scalar values from higher scopes override; array-valued settings (permission rules, allowlists) concatenate and deduplicate across scopes rather than replace. A deny at any level cannot be overridden by another level.

nodes.label: Hook configuration (allowManagedHooksOnly / allowedHttpHookUrls)
nodes.rationale: allowManagedHooksOnly (managed-only) blocks user/project/other-plugin hooks, loading only managed/SDK/force-enabled-plugin hooks so admins distribute vetted hooks via an org marketplace. allowedHttpHookUrls and httpHookAllowedEnvVars restrict HTTP hook targets and interpolated env vars; arrays merge across sources.

nodes.label: When edits take effect (settings reload)
nodes.rationale: Claude Code watches settings files and reloads permissions/hooks/apiKeyHelper without a restart; the ConfigChange hook fires for each detected change. model and outputStyle are read once at session start.

nodes.label: strictPluginOnlyCustomization
nodes.rationale: Managed-only setting that blocks skills/agents/hooks/MCP servers from user and project sources so they can only come from plugins or managed settings; combine with strictKnownMarketplaces to control the full customization supply chain. Requires v2.1.82+.

nodes.label: Local stdio MCP server (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR)
nodes.rationale: Claude Code sets CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR in a spawned stdio server's environment to the project root — the same directory hooks receive — so servers resolve project-relative paths without depending on cwd.

nodes.label: MCP tool naming (mcp__server__tool)
nodes.rationale: MCP server tools appear as regular tools named mcp__<server>__<tool>, so hook matchers target them like any other tool. MCP prompts surface as /mcp__servername__promptname commands.

nodes.label: MCP elicitation
nodes.rationale: MCP servers request structured input mid-task (form or URL mode); the Elicitation hook can auto-respond without showing a dialog.

nodes.label: Plugin hooks component
nodes.rationale: Plugins provide event handlers in hooks/hooks.json (or inline in plugin.json) responding to the same lifecycle events as user-defined hooks. Bundled scripts referenced via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; changes to hooks/ require /reload-plugins or restart to pick up.

nodes.label: Plugin userConfig
nodes.rationale: Declares values prompted at enable time, substitutable as ${user_config.KEY} in hook commands and exported as CLAUDE_PLUGIN_OPTION_<KEY>; sensitive values go to the system keychain instead of settings.json.

nodes.label: Persistent data directory (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA)
nodes.rationale: Resolves to ~/.claude/plugins/data/{id}/ and survives plugin updates, unlike CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT. Recommended pattern: a SessionStart hook diffs the bundled manifest against a stored copy to reinstall dependencies only when an update changes them.

nodes.label: .worktreeinclude
nodes.rationale: Copies gitignored files (e.g. .env) into new worktrees using .gitignore syntax; only gitignored matches are copied so tracked files are never duplicated. Not processed when a WorktreeCreate hook replaces the default git behavior — copy local config inside the hook script instead.

nodes.label: security-guidance plugin (hook-based review)
nodes.rationale: Built entirely on hooks: registers SessionStart (bootstrap), UserPromptSubmit (capture baseline), PostToolUse on Edit/Write/NotebookEdit (per-edit pattern match), Stop (end-of-turn diff review), and PostToolUse on Bash filtered to git commit/push (commit review). Runs a separate Claude review with fresh context so the writer does not grade itself; findings are fed back as instructions but do not block — defense in depth, not a guarantee. For hard enforcement pair with a hook that blocks the edit.

nodes.label: Per-edit security patterns (security-patterns.yaml)
nodes.rationale: Deterministic string/regex match with no model call (no usage cost); custom rules added via .claude/security-patterns.yaml with rule_name/reminder/regex/substrings/paths fields. Additive only — a rule cannot disable built-in checks. Loads up to 50 rules, skips regexes prone to catastrophic backtracking.

nodes.label: /goal command
nodes.rationale: A wrapper around a session-scoped prompt-based Stop hook: after each turn the condition plus conversation is sent to a small fast model (Haiku default) that returns yes/no with a reason, so completion is decided by a fresh model rather than the one doing the work. Unavailable when disableAllHooks or allowManagedHooksOnly is set, because the evaluator is part of the hooks system.

nodes.label: Status Line stdin JSON Data Contract
nodes.norm_label: status line stdin json data contract

hyperedges.label: PreToolUse hook + permission rule deny-first precedence
